seo.title: 100 Privacy-First Architecture resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Privacy-First Architecture resources for engineers.

content.intro: Building privacy-first architecture requires shifting from a 'collect everything' mindset to a data minimization strategy. For European developers and privacy-focused SaaS founders, this involves selecting tools that offer data residency in the EU, avoid cross-site tracking, and allow for granular control over PII (Personally Identifiable Information). This guide provides a curated list of tools and architectural patterns to implement privacy by design without sacrificing essential functionality.

items.title: Plausible Analytics
items.description: A lightweight, open-source alternative to Google Analytics that does not use cookies and is fully compliant with GDPR, CCPA, and PECR out of the box.

items.title: Umami
items.description: Self-hosted web analytics solution that provides a single script to track page views and events without collecting identifiable user data.

items.title: Fathom Analytics
items.description: Privacy-centric analytics that uses a custom routing complex to bypass ad-blockers while maintaining compliance through anonymized data processing.

items.title: PostHog (Self-Hosted)
items.description: Deploy PostHog via Docker or Kubernetes on your own infrastructure to keep event data and session recordings within your private network.

items.title: Matomo On-Premise
items.description: The industry standard for self-hosted analytics, offering 100% data ownership and a comprehensive feature set including heatmaps and A/B testing.

items.title: Simple Analytics
items.description: A clean analytics dashboard that never stores IP addresses or uses cookies, targeting developers who want zero-configuration compliance.

items.title: Ackee
items.description: Node.js based analytics tool that provides a GraphQL API, allowing you to build custom privacy-first dashboards on top of your data.

items.title: Shynet
items.description: Modern, privacy-focused web analytics that works without cookies or JS tracking pixels, utilizing server-side request analysis.

items.title: GoatCounter
items.description: Open-source web statistics service that can be used as a hosted service or self-hosted, focusing on simplicity and non-intrusive tracking.

items.title: Offen Fair Web Analytics
items.description: An analytics tool that requires explicit user opt-in before any data is collected, giving users full access to the data stored about them.

items.title: Hetzner Cloud (EU Regions)
items.description: Deploy infrastructure in Falkenstein or Helsinki to ensure data remains under EU jurisdiction, avoiding the reach of the US CLOUD Act.

items.title: Scaleway Elements
items.description: A European cloud provider offering S3-compatible storage and managed Kubernetes with strict adherence to European data sovereignty laws.

items.title: Supabase (Self-Hosted)
items.description: Use the Docker-compose setup to host your own Firebase alternative, ensuring all user authentication and database records stay on your hardware.

items.title: PlanetScale Data Branching
items.description: Use branching to create isolated development environments with masked production data to prevent PII leaks to developer machines.

items.title: HashiCorp Vault
items.description: Implement Transit Secret Engine to encrypt PII at the application layer before it ever reaches your primary database storage.

items.title: Cloak Engine
items.description: A specialized middleware for transparently encrypting database fields, ensuring that even if the DB is compromised, the data remains unreadable.

items.title: In-Memory Data Masking
items.description: Implement application logic to redact sensitive fields from logs and error reporting tools like Sentry or LogRocket using custom filters.

items.title: MinIO
items.description: High-performance object storage that you can host on-premise to replace AWS S3 for sensitive user uploads and documents.

items.title: Cryptomator (Server-Side)
items.description: Integrate client-side encryption for files uploaded by users, ensuring the server host never has access to the raw file content.

items.title: MariaDB ColumnStore for Anonymization
items.description: Utilize columnar storage for large-scale analytical queries on anonymized datasets to separate operational PII from business intelligence.

items.title: Ollama
items.description: Run Large Language Models locally on your own servers to process user data without sending it to OpenAI or Anthropic APIs.

items.title: LocalAI
items.description: A self-hosted, OpenAI-compatible API that acts as a drop-in replacement for LLM tasks, keeping all processing within your local infrastructure.

items.title: Buttondown
items.description: A privacy-first newsletter tool that strips tracking pixels from emails and provides a clean, privacy-respecting subscription flow.

items.title: Listmonk
items.description: Self-hosted newsletter and mailing list manager with a high-performance backend, allowing full control over subscriber data and mail delivery.

items.title: Matrix / Synapse
items.description: Implement the Matrix protocol for end-to-end encrypted messaging between users or for internal team communications.

items.title: Signal Protocol Integration
items.description: Incorporate the Signal library for end-to-end encryption in custom chat applications to ensure zero-knowledge of message content.

items.title: Jitsi Meet (Self-Hosted)
items.description: Deploy your own video conferencing server to avoid third-party monitoring of metadata and call recordings.

items.title: CryptPad
items.description: An open-source alternative to Google Docs that uses end-to-end encryption for collaborative editing and document storage.

items.title: Bitwarden (Self-Hosted)
items.description: Use the open-source Vaultwarden implementation to host a password and secret manager for your development team on your own hardware.

items.title: Proton Mail Bridge
items.description: Integrate Proton’s encrypted email service into your automated transactional mail workflows using the local IMAP/SMTP bridge.

pro_tips: Always use IP hashing with a daily rotating salt for analytics to ensure you cannot reconstruct a user's identity over long periods.
Implement a 'Data TTL' (Time To Live) policy at the database level to automatically purge sensitive user data that is no longer required for business operations.
Prefer European-owned IaaS providers like Hetzner or Scaleway over US-owned providers with EU regions to minimize risks from the US CLOUD Act.
Proxy all third-party scripts (like analytics or fonts) through your own domain to prevent third-party providers from collecting user IP addresses and headers.
Use 'Privacy Manifests' in your application code to document exactly why every piece of data is being collected, facilitating easier compliance audits.
